steps.title: Trace the step sequence
steps.content: Solis 'starts by' creating the base, then [blank] builds the walls by layering coils, 'then' smooths the walls with a hacksaw blade, and 'at last' the pot is painted. The passage walks through pottery-making stages in order.

steps.title: Pick a sequence marker
steps.content: The blank introduces the second stage in a forward-moving procedure, so a transition meaning 'the following step' is needed.

steps.title: Eliminate by choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — For example,: would mark building the walls as an illustration of making the base, but it is a separate later step, not an example. ✗
  • Choice B — However,: signals contrast, but building the walls does not oppose making the base. ✗
  • Choice C — By contrast,: also signals contrast between two ideas, which does not fit a continuous procedure. ✗
  • Choice D — Next,: marks the wall-building as the following step in the sequence, fitting 'starts by... [next]... then... at last.' ✓
That is the correct answer.
